pattern: Grouping bids by item, one active bid per user, turns 'who is winning' into a scan over a small per-item group rather than a search over every bid ever placed, with ties broken by the more recent (higher) user id.
hints: Bids only matter per item, and each user contributes at most one live bid to a given item, so nest the storage by item first and by user second instead of keeping one flat list.
addBid, updateBid, and removeBid are all just writes or deletes on that per-item, per-user map — the interesting work is entirely in getHighestBidder.
getHighestBidder walks the current bids for that item looking for the largest amount, and among equal amounts keeps the larger userId; an item with no live bids at all returns -1.

edge_inputs.description: sample: outbid, update, remove, and querying an item with no bids at all
addBid called twice by the same user on the same item replaces the earlier bid rather than adding a second one
equal bid amounts on the same item are broken by the higher userId
removing the current leader hands the win to the next-highest remaining bid
an updateBid can push a trailing bidder into the lead
userId, itemId, and bidAmount at the constraint extremes
bids on separate items never interact, even for the same users and amounts
querying the highest bidder for an item that has never received a bid

stress.description: random addBid/updateBid/removeBid/getHighestBidder that only updates or removes bids known to be live
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    ops = ['AuctionSystem']
    args = [[]]
    live = {}
    users = list(range(1, 12))
    items = list(range(1, 6))
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        r = rng.random()
        if r < 0.45:
            u = rng.choice(users)
            it = rng.choice(items)
            amt = rng.randint(1, 500)
            ops.append('addBid')
            args.append([u, it, amt])
            live[(u, it)] = amt
        elif r < 0.7 and live:
            key = rng.choice(list(live.keys()))
            u, it = key
            amt = rng.randint(1, 500)
            ops.append('updateBid')
            args.append([u, it, amt])
            live[key] = amt
        elif r < 0.85 and live:
            key = rng.choice(list(live.keys()))
            u, it = key
            ops.append('removeBid')
            args.append([u, it])
            del live[key]
        else:
            it = rng.choice(items)
            ops.append('getHighestBidder')
            args.append([it])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: generator tracks its own (userId, itemId) -> live-bid map so updateBid/removeBid only ever target an existing bid, matching the problem's guarantee

complexity.time: O(bids on the item) per getHighestBidder, O(1) otherwise
